figures.caption: Figure 1: Schematic of workflow of the present forcing-informed (FI) resolvent analysis, shown for an example of a two-dimensional cylinder case. Detailed mathematical formulations are presented in §2.3 and §3.

Figure 2: Time trajectories of the state vector 𝑞′ (blue) and the nonlinear forcing vector 𝑓 ′NL (orange) of the Stuart–Landau oscillator. (a) The first components 𝑞′1 and 𝑓 ′NL,1 from the initial condition to the limit cycle oscillation (LCO) at 0 ≤ 𝑡 ≤ 120. (b) All components at 152 ≤ 𝑡 ≤ 161 in the LCO domain. Solid lines, the first components of 𝑞′ and 𝑓 ′NL; dashed lines, the second components.

Figure 3: Results for the Stuart-Landau oscillator. (a) Eigenvalue 𝜆𝑟 + i𝜆𝑖 of 𝑳data obtained by the proposed method (red circle) and by DMD (blue triangle) and the true eigenvalue (𝜆𝑟 , 𝜆𝑖) = (0.1, 2.0) (black cross). The red circles collapse onto the true eigenvalue regardless of the time ranges. (b) Leading singular value 𝜎1 of proposed fully data-driven FI resolvent operator 𝑮FDD (orange circle) and conventional resolvent operator 𝑹 (gray plus), and square roots of the leading SPOD gains (red cross).

Figure 4: Time evolutions of ⟨𝑞′, 𝑳𝑞′⟩ (solid blue line), ⟨𝑞′, 𝑓 ′NL⟩ (solid orange line), and their sum (solid green line), for the Stuart-Landau system. The dashed blue/orange line is the time-averaged energy supply/removal due to the linear/nonlinear mechanisms during the LCO, evaluated using the leading FI response 𝑞𝐺,1 and forcing modes 𝑓𝐺,1.

Figure 5: Data-driven estimate of linear time-invariant operator 𝑳data of two-dimensional cylinder flow. (a) Eigenvalues 𝜆 = 𝜆𝑟 + i𝜆𝑖 of the proposed 𝑳data (green and magenta circles) and the most unstable eigenvalue of 𝑳 computed in operator-based approach (black cross), (b) real part of the streamwise momentum component of the eigenvector ?̂?𝑥 of 𝑳data corresponding to the most unstable eigenvalue indicated by the magenta circle.

Figure 6: Leading gains (singular values 𝜎1) for 2D cylinder case, obtained from the fully data-driven 𝑮FDD (orange circle), semi-data-driven 𝑮SDD (blue triangle), and the ordinary resolvent operator 𝑹 (gray plus), along with the secondary leading gains of 𝜎2 (𝑮FDD)(orange cross). The square roots of the leading eigenvalues SPOD gains 𝑺qq(red cross) are also shown.

Figure 7: Spatial distributions of the leading FI response and forcing modes in 2D cylinder wake extracted by the fully data-driven approach using 𝑮FDD. Real parts of streamwise momentum components are shown for the FI response 𝑞𝑚𝑥 /∥𝑞𝑚𝑥 ∥∞ (top) and forcing 𝑓𝑚𝑥 /∥ 𝑓𝑚𝑥 ∥∞ (middle). The nonlinear energy transfer map for the streamwise momentum component T𝑚𝑥 := Re[𝒁𝑞∗𝑚𝑥 ⊙ 𝒁 𝑓𝑚𝑥 ], scaled by its infinity norm ∥T𝑚𝑥 ∥∞, is shown at the bottom.
Figure 8: Conventional resolvent modes obtained using 𝑹: streamwise component of (a) response mode, (b) forcing mode. The real part of the momentum component scaled by its infinity norm is shown.

Figure 9: Phase evolutions of the FI (a) response and (b) forcing modes at 𝑆𝑡𝐾 , along with the (normalized) streamwise component of phase velocity of FI (c) response and (d) forcing modes. In (b), the black arrows indicate the direction of the movement of the FI forcing mode structure. Spatial distributions of−∇∠𝑞𝐺,𝑚𝑥 /∥∇∠𝑞𝐺,𝑚𝑥 ∥ and−∇∠ 𝑓𝐺,𝑚𝑥 /∥∇∠ 𝑓𝐺,𝑚𝑥 ∥ are shown in (c) and (d), respectively. The gray isoline of 𝑢 = 0 indicates the recirculation domain.
Figure 10: Same as figure 7 but for semi-data-driven approach using 𝑮SDD.

Figure 11: Topview of the instantaneous iso-surfaces of Q criterion 𝑄/(𝑎∞/𝑥0)2 = 25 colored by the height from the wall 𝑌 := 𝑦/𝑥0 of the DNS data [Iwatani et al., 2025], where 𝑥0 is the reference length. The magenta rectangle domain is used for the FI resolvent analysis. Here, 𝑋 := (𝑥 − 𝑥𝑑)/𝑥0 and 𝑍 := 𝑧/Λ𝑧 , where 𝑥𝑑 is the disturbance slot location and 𝜆𝑧 is the wavelength of the subharmonic initial disturbance.

Figure 12: Late stage of H-type transition, visualized by iso-surfaces of Q criterion (𝑄/(𝑎∞/𝑥0)2 = 10) colored by spanwise velocity 𝑤: (a) oblique view of the whole analysis domain (copied once in the spanwise direction 𝑍), and (b) front and (c) top views near the perpendicularly standing ring-vortex (PSRV) denoted in black rectangle in (a).
Figure 13: Eigenvalues 𝜆𝑟 + i𝜆𝑖 of the estimated linear operator 𝑳data obtained using 𝑁POD = 10, 90, 170, 250: (a) overall view; (b) enlarged view near unstable eigenvalues.

Figure 14: Isosurfaces of momentum components ?̂? for the most unstable eigenmodes of the H-type transitional boundary layer (𝜆𝑖/𝜔2𝐷 ≈ 3/2), computed from the data-driven linear operator 𝑳data with 𝑁pod = 90: (left) streamwise ?̂?𝑥 , (middle) wall-normal ?̂?𝑦 , (right) spanwise ?̂?𝑧 momentum components. For visualization, the iso-surfaces are copied once in the spanwise direction.
Figure 15: Gains, 𝜎𝐺,1 and 𝜎𝐺,2, of the FI resolvent operator 𝑮FDD for the transition boundary layer case, compared with the square root of the leading SPOD gains 𝜆1/2 1 (𝑺qq).

Figure 16: Results of FI resolvent analysis of transitional boundary layer for streamwise momentum component at (a) 𝜔/𝜔2𝐷 = 1/2 and (b) 2. Topviews of iso-surfaces of the FI response mode 𝑞𝑚𝑥 /|𝑞𝑚𝑥 |∞ = ±0.25 (top), the FI forcing mode 𝑓𝑚𝑥 /∥ 𝑓𝑚𝑥 ∥∞ = ±0.25 (middle), and the nonlinear energy transfer map T𝑚𝑥 /∥T𝑚𝑥 ∥∞ = ±0.25 in (a) and 0.1 in (b)(bottom). Red and green indicate the positive iso-values, whereas blue and purple indicate the negative iso-values.

Figure 17: Input-output relations at 𝑋 ≈ 4.5 where a perpendicularly standing ring-like vortex (PSRV) is observed. Streamwise momentum component of the FI response and forcing modes at 𝜔/𝜔2𝐷 = 1/2 (left) and 2 (middle). Snapshots of streamwise momentum component of the output 𝑚′𝑥 and nonlinear forcing 𝑓 ′𝑚𝑥 (right).

Figure 18: Comparison between the eigenmode 𝑚𝑥 (global stability mode) of 𝑳data using 𝑁POD = 90 and nonlinear energy transfer map 𝑻𝑚𝑥 , for streamwise momentum component, at 𝜔/𝜔2𝐷 = 1/2 and 2. Eigenvalues 𝜆𝑟 + i𝜆𝑖 of 𝑳data indicate the stability of the corresponding eigenmodes.

Figure 19: Comparison between the present nonlinear energy transfer maps computed from the most dominant FI response and forcing modes (top: fully data-driven approach; middle: semi-data-driven approach) and the map based on DNS data (bottom) in Jin et al. [2021] (Adapted with permission from Jin et al. [2021]. Copyright (2021) by American Physical Society).
